title: Air-conditioning and refrigeration installers and repairers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Test electrical circuits or components for continuity using electrical test equipment.
Recommend, develop, or perform preventive or general maintenance procedures, such as cleaning, washing, or vacuuming equipment, oiling parts, or changing filters.
Record and report time, materials, faults, deficiencies, or other unusual occurrences on work orders.
Inspect and test systems to verify compliance with plans and specifications or to detect malfunctions.
Lay out and connect electrical wiring between controls and equipment according to wiring diagrams, using hand tools.
Braise or solder parts to repair defective joints and leaks, and perform repairs and replacements on equipment, components, or wiring as necessary.

Install auxiliary components to heating or cooling equipment, such as valves, ducts, pipes, and blowers, and connect heating or air conditioning equipment to fuel, water, or refrigerant sources to form complete circuits.
Study blueprints, design specifications, or manufacturers' recommendations to ascertain the configuration of heating or cooling equipment components and ensure proper installation.
Comply with all applicable standards, policies, or procedures, such as safety procedures and maintaining a clean work area.
Install, connect, or adjust thermostats, humidistats, or timers and install dehumidifiers for spaces requiring cool, dry air.
Discuss system malfunctions with users to isolate problems or to verify that repairs have corrected malfunctions.
Adjust system controls to settings recommended by the manufacturer to balance the system and install control valves using appropriate tools.
Cut or drill holes in structures to install equipment, using power tools, and mount components in specified locations using hand tools.
Measure, cut, thread, or bend pipe or tubing using appropriate tools.
Keep records of repairs, replacements made, and causes of malfunctions.
Install or repair air purification systems, including specialized filters or UV light systems, and conduct efficiency improvements on HVAC systems by changing filters and cleaning ducts.
Estimate, order, pick up, deliver, and install materials and supplies needed to maintain equipment in good working condition and schedule work with customers.
Supervise and instruct assistants in their tasks.
Lay out reference points for installation of structural and functional components using measuring instruments.
Lift and align components into position using hoists or block and tackle.
Install or repair ground source heat pumps to minimize energy consumption and reduce carbon emissions.
Perform mechanical overhauls and refrigerant reclaiming for HVAC systems.
Monitor, inspect, or test mechanical equipment and perform routine maintenance to ensure operational readiness.
Document repairs and maintenance conducted, including test results when applicable.
Analyze performance of systems based on testing to improve fuel efficiency or functionality.
Prepare equipment inspection schedules and reliability records for equipment management.
Conduct failure analyses, document results, and recommend corrective actions for defective systems.
Fabricate or assemble mechanical, electrical, or electronic components or assemblies, and maintain documentation of such activities.
Install electrical or electronic parts and hardware in systems to ensure proper function, interpreting technical instructions, blueprints, and schematics.
Repair, modify, and calibrate components and systems, ensuring compliance with operational requirements.
Train customers or personnel to install, use, or maintain HVAC systems effectively.

Formulate conceptual design and develop specifications for aeronautical or aerospace products or systems to meet customer requirements, evaluate performance of craft during trials, and ensure compliance with engineering principles, environmental regulations, and quality standards.
Design and analyze agricultural and marine machinery components, systems, and structures, including conducting tests to ensure adequate performance and compliance with specifications.
Investigate equipment failures or difficulties, diagnose performance problems, and recommend remedial actions for marine machinery and equipment.
Prepare technical reports and documentation, including product specifications, test results, and engineering proposals for use by engineering and management personnel.
Coordinate activities with regulatory bodies and other departments to ensure the efficient installation, operation, maintenance, or repair of equipment and systems, while minimizing costs.
Perform research and development activities related to vehicle and marine systems, including alternative fuel technologies, energy efficiency, and new engineering methods or designs.
Evaluate mechanical designs or prototypes for energy performance or environmental impact and provide technical consultation on engineering projects.
Conduct system-level testing, analysis, or research to develop, validate, or improve automotive and aerospace systems and technologies.
Develop and implement operating methods, procedures, and maintenance schedules for machines and equipment to maximize efficiency and performance.
Maintain records and reports related to engineering activities, including performance evaluations and maintenance schedules.
Participate in the design or modification of systems and proposals to enhance performance and safety of vehicles and machinery.
Discuss plans with clients and contractors to evaluate and modify designs as necessary for agricultural or marine projects.
Adjust or maintain equipment, such as lasers, microscopes, oscilloscopes, or energy measurement devices, and perform skilled repair or maintenance operations using tools and equipment.
Conduct, interpret, or evaluate non-destructive testing methods, such as liquid penetrant tests, ultrasonic testing, or thermal testing, and document testing processes and results.
Monitor inventory levels, order supplies as needed, and requisition materials and tools to ensure adequate working conditions.
Maintain clean working environments according to clean room standards and inspect work areas for safety compliance.
Prepare reports and document procedures related to maintenance, testing results, and operations.
Develop and implement maintenance policies and procedures, including worker training for operational safety and repair techniques.
Evaluate equipment and systems to determine needed repairs, and recommend changes to equipment design or materials for cost reduction.
Supervise or coordinate the work of staff and trainees, monitoring performance and performing assessments of work quality.
Confer with personnel and conduct meetings with vendors to discuss repair work and equipment needs.
Meet safety standards while mixing or using processing chemicals or gases as necessary.
Review and evaluate completed work from contractors and participate in budget preparation to monitor departmental expenditures.
Inspect and test completed work against specifications and contractual requirements using hand tools or gauges.
Assist scientists or engineers with photonic experiments and test or perform failure analysis for optomechanical or optoelectrical products according to test plans.
Optimize photonic process parameters and develop new non-destructive testing methods and techniques.
Compile operational or personnel records, such as repair or maintenance statistics, and document non-destructive testing methods and results.

emerging_tasks.task: Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Communicate results in peer-reviewed research articles or at workshops or conferences.
Develop and test autonomous systems for uncrewed aerospace vehicles.
Develop plans for safely drydocking vessels, including structural integrity, block loading, and stability.
Investigate equipment failures or diagnose faulty operations and recommend or perform remedial actions.
Use agricultural drones for crop monitoring, irrigation management, and pest control.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
